steps.description: Click the language selection button (flag icon) in the top-right corner of the VS Code "Welcome" sign-in dialog.
Press **Ctrl + `** to open the integrated terminal in Visual Studio Code from the main workspace window.
Type the text `export TEAMSFX_BUILDER_API=true` into the integrated bash terminal at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace.
Press the Enter key in the VS Code integrated terminal to execute the command `export TEAMSFX_BUILDER_API=true`.
Type the text `export TEAMSFX_EMBEDDED_KNOWLEDGE=true` into the Bash terminal in the bottom panel of Visual Studio Code, within the workspace directory.
Press the Enter key in the integrated terminal of Visual Studio Code after entering the command `export TEAMSFX_EMBEDDED_KNOWLEDGE=true` to execute it.
Type the text 'code .' into the integrated Terminal (bash) at the bottom of the Visual Studio Code workspace.
Press the Enter key in the integrated Terminal of Visual Studio Code after typing "code ".
Click the language selection button (showing an English flag) at the bottom left of the VS Code welcome screen to change the interface language.
Click the "Source Control" icon in the left sidebar of the Visual Studio Code interface to open the source control panel.

Click the "Use weaker encryption" button in the keyring warning dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code window. This button appears when VS Code cannot identify an OS keyring for storing encryption-related data.

Click the "Create an Agent/App" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit panel on the Visual Studio Code welcome screen. The button is visually highlighted in blue, located on the left sidebar, and initiates the creation of a new agent or app.
Click the "Declarative Agent" option in the dropdown under the "New Project" dialog.
Click the "No Action" entry in the dropdown menu under the "Add an action to your declarative agent" header, located within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Default folder" dropdown in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to set the project root folder path (`/home/.vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects`).
Click the "New File" button at the top of the Explorer sidebar in Visual Studio Code to create a new file in the workspace.
Click the "Add Capability" option under the DEVELOPMENT section in the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel of Visual Studio Code.
Click the "Embedded Knowledge" option in the "Add Capability" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dialog within Visual Studio Code's "Select Teams manifest.json File" interface.
Click the "Browse..." button in the "Select files" dialog for adding embedded knowledge files within the Visual Studio Code extension interface.
Click the breadcrumb navigation item labeled "vscode" at the top of the file chooser dialog to navigate up one folder in the directory path.
Click on the "additional-files" folder entry in the file picker dialog under the "Home" directory, within the application’s open file interface.
Press the Enter key to open the selected "additional-files" folder in the file picker dialog of a Linux desktop application.
Click the "azure.js" file name in the file picker dialog to select it.
Press the Enter key to open the selected file "azure.json" in the Linux file chooser dialog.
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code to confirm adding a capability.
Click the "Zip App Package" option in the UTILITY section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "manifest.json" file entry in the file selection dialog within Visual Studio Code, specifically under the "Select Teams manifest.json File" popup, to choose the manifest file.
Click the “dev” environment option in the “Select an environment” dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, targeting the Microsoft 365 Agents extension.
Click the "Zip App Package" option in the UTILITY section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code.
Press **Ctrl+Shift+P** to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code while the "MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT" side panel is active.
Type the text 'add capability' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top of the interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Add Capability" command in the Visual Studio Code command palette, located at the top center of the interface, after searching for ">add capability".
Click the "Embedded Knowledge" dropdown option in the "Add Capability" dialog within Visual Studio Code, selecting the capability source for grounding information.
Click the "manifest.json" file option in the file selection dropdown within Visual Studio Code when prompted to select a Teams manifest file.
Click the "Browse..." button in the "Add embedded knowledge files" file selection dialog within Visual Studio Code.
Click the "vscode" breadcrumb folder in the file picker navigation bar to navigate up one directory within the file selection dialog.
Click the "addition-files" folder entry in the file selection dialog of a Linux desktop file chooser, under the "Home" location, to select it for opening.
Press the Enter key to open the selected folder "additional-files" in the file picker dialog.
Click the "azure.parameters.test.json" file entry in the file selection dialog to select it for opening.
Press the Enter key to select and open the highlighted file "azure.parameters.test.json" in the Linux file picker dialog within a vscode workspace directory.
Click the "Add" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit confirmation dialog within Visual Studio Code, located in the popup at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Zip App Package" option in the UTILITY section of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT panel within Visual Studio Code to build the app into a package for publishing.
Click the "manifest.json" file option in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within Visual Studio Code, highlighting the file for selection.
Click the "dev" option in the "Select an environment" dropdown list within the Visual Studio Code Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" icon in the Visual Studio Code sidebar to open the toolkit extension panel.
Click the "appPackage" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to expand its contents.
Click the "declarativeAgent.json" file in the "appPackage" folder within the Visual Studio Code Explorer panel to open the file.
@assertion "EmbeddedKnowledge/azure.json" and "EmbeddedKnowledge/azure.parameters.test.json" exists in the file "declarativeAgent.json"
Click the "EmbeddedKnowledge" folder in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code, within the "appPackage" directory.
@assertion the files "azure.json" and "azure.parameters.test.json" under the folder "EmbeddedKnowledge" of the folder "appPackage"
